core_mechanism: computing the Shannon information-channel capacity of a channel with signal-dependent (multiplicative) noise, obtained by recasting a nonlinear transmission channel as a linear channel with multiplicative noise, which yields a capacity that is non-monotonic in input power and saturates at a finite maximum rather than growing without bound as in the additive-noise case
brief_reason: The abstract's key step is relating the nonlinear channel to a linear channel with multiplicative noise to derive its information capacity analytically, finding a maximal capacity value in fundamental distinction to the ever-growing capacity of the additive-noise linear channel.
